title: Pteris × duvalii
intro: Pteris × duvalii, also known as Duval's fern, is a sterile hybrid species of the Pteris fern genus, characterized by its unique frond structure and growth habit. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the management of various health conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pteris × duvalii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for wound healing and skin care.
taxonomy: Pteris × duvalii is a nothospecies of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It is a hybrid of Pteris cretica and Pteris multifida. This hybrid fern is native to the Mediterranean region. Pteris × duvalii is a popular ornamental plant due to its attractive foliage and adaptability to various environments. The classification of Pteris × duvalii as a nothospecies indicates its artificial creation through hybridization.
